prompt: Build the schedule from the dependencies: A starts the project; B and C both start once A finishes; D starts only after BOTH B and C finish. Each bar is pre-sized to its duration — drag it to its correct start week.
caption: Finish-to-start dependencies. Drag each bar; it snaps to a week.

bars.label: 'A'
bars.why: 'A has no predecessor — it starts in week 1.'

bars.label: 'B'
bars.why: 'B follows A (finishes end of week 2) → starts week 3.'

bars.label: 'C'
bars.why: 'C also follows A → starts week 3.'

bars.label: 'D'
bars.why: 'D waits for B and C (latest finish is end of week 4) → starts week 5.'

explanation: Forward pass: A (wk 1–2) → B and C (wk 3–4 / wk 3) → D (wk 5–6). The critical path A-B-D drives the 6-week finish; C has one week of float.

prompt: Build the schedule: Plan comes first; Dev and Docs both start after Plan finishes; Deploy starts only after BOTH Dev and Docs finish. Drag each pre-sized bar to its correct start week.
caption: Finish-to-start dependencies. Drag each bar; it snaps to a week.

bars.label: 'Plan'
bars.why: 'Plan starts the project — week 1.'

bars.label: 'Dev'
bars.why: 'Dev follows Plan (finishes end of week 1) → starts week 2, runs to week 4.'

bars.label: 'Docs'
bars.why: 'Docs also follows Plan → starts week 2, runs to week 3.'

bars.label: 'Deploy'
bars.why: 'Deploy waits for Dev (end wk 4) and Docs (end wk 3) → starts week 5.'

explanation: Forward pass: Plan (wk 1) → Dev (wk 2–4) & Docs (wk 2–3) → Deploy (wk 5–6). Critical path Plan-Dev-Deploy = 6 weeks; Docs has a week of float.

prompt: Read the activity network above: each box is an activity and its duration in weeks; arrows are finish-to-start dependencies. Do the forward pass and drag each task bar to its EARLIEST start week.
caption: Network → Gantt. A starts the project; D waits for BOTH B and C.

bars.label: 'A'
bars.why: 'A has no predecessor — earliest start is week 1.'

bars.label: 'B'
bars.why: 'B follows A (ends week 2) → earliest start week 3, runs to week 5.'

bars.label: 'C'
bars.why: 'C follows A → earliest start week 3 (just 1 week).'

bars.label: 'D'
bars.why: 'D waits for BOTH B (ends wk 5) and C (ends wk 3) — the later is week 5, so D starts week 6.'

explanation: Forward pass: A (wk 1–2) → B (wk 3–5) and C (wk 3) → D starts after the later predecessor (B, wk 5) so D is wk 6–7. Critical path A-B-D = 7 weeks; C carries 2 weeks of float.

prompt: Build the schedule from the dependencies: Charter first; Design and Procure both follow Charter; Docs follows Design; Build follows BOTH Design and Procure; Test follows Build. Drag each pre-sized bar to its earliest start week.
caption: Six tasks, two parallel branches. Some tasks carry float.

bars.label: 'Charter'
bars.why: 'No predecessor — starts week 1.'

bars.label: 'Design'
bars.why: 'Follows Charter (ends wk 1) → starts week 2, runs to week 4.'

bars.label: 'Procure'
bars.why: 'Also follows Charter → starts week 2, runs to week 3.'

bars.label: 'Docs'
bars.why: 'Follows Design (ends wk 4) → starts week 5; runs parallel to Build, with float.'

bars.label: 'Build'
bars.why: 'Waits for BOTH Design (wk 4) and Procure (wk 3) → starts week 5, runs to week 7.'

bars.label: 'Test'
bars.why: 'Follows Build (ends wk 7) → starts week 8, ends week 9.'

explanation: Critical path Charter-Design-Build-Test = 1+3+3+2 = 9 weeks. Procure finishes before Build needs it (1 week float); Docs runs alongside Build (also has float). The critical path is the longest chain, with zero float.

prompt: Tasks run back-to-back; the diamonds (◆) are milestones — zero-duration markers. Drag each task bar to its start week, and each milestone to the point in time it marks.
caption: Bars have duration; diamonds mark a single point.

bars.label: 'Kickoff'
bars.why: 'The kickoff milestone marks the project start — the very beginning (week 1).'

bars.label: 'Design'
bars.why: 'Begins at kickoff → week 1, runs three weeks to week 3.'

bars.label: 'Build'
bars.why: 'Follows Design (ends week 3) → starts week 4, runs to week 5.'

bars.label: 'Test'
bars.why: 'Follows Build (ends week 5) → starts week 6, runs to week 7.'

bars.label: 'Go-live'
bars.why: 'The go-live milestone marks completion — the point Test finishes (end of week 7).'

explanation: Milestones are zero-duration points, not spans: Kickoff sits at the very start; Go-live sits where the last task (Test) finishes. The task bars run back-to-back — Design (wk 1–3) → Build (wk 4–5) → Test (wk 6–7).
